title: Add 1Password and 1password-cli to Brewfile
description: 1Password is a password manager with a CLI (op) that integrates with direnv for secrets injection and SSH agent for key management. Add 'cask 1password' and 'brew 1password-cli' to .Brewfile. The CLI enables 'op run --' prefix for secret injection in dev workflows.

title: Add macOS system defaults script
description: Create scripts/macos-defaults.sh with idempotent defaults write commands for key repeat speed, Finder settings (show hidden files, path bar, sort folders first), Dock (autohide, tile size), screenshot location (~/Screenshots), and disabling .DS_Store on network volumes. Document that it requires re-login or killall for some settings to take effect. Wire into install.sh as an optional step or run manually.

title: Add ~/.ssh/config template to dotfiles
description: An SSH config template is not tracked. Add dotfiles/.ssh/config with common settings: ServerAliveInterval, AddKeysToAgent, UseKeychain (macOS), IdentityFile defaults, and commented placeholder host entries. Symlink via install.sh with appropriate 600 permissions handling.

title: Add karabiner-elements config to dotfiles
description: karabiner-elements is installed and in use but its config (~/.config/karabiner/karabiner.json) is not tracked. Add to dotfiles as dotfiles/.config/karabiner/karabiner.json and symlink via install.sh. Preserves key remapping rules across reinstalls.

title: Add .gitignore_global to dotfiles
description: A global gitignore is not currently tracked. Add dotfiles/.gitignore_global covering: .DS_Store, .envrc, *.pyc, __pycache__, .venv, node_modules, .mise.toml local overrides, editor swap files (.swp, .swo), and OS/IDE artifacts. Set core.excludesfile in .gitconfig to point to it.

title: Add .gitconfig to dotfiles
description: A global .gitconfig is not currently tracked in the bootstrap repo. Add dotfiles/.gitconfig with: user identity, default branch (main), push behavior (simple or current), rebase-on-pull, delta as core.pager, common aliases (lg, st, co, etc.), and merge/diff tool settings. Symlink via install.sh.

title: Add mise to Brewfile and evaluate replacing pyenv/nvm/rbenv
description: mise is a unified runtime version manager that replaces pyenv, nvm, rbenv, and others with a single tool and .mise.toml config files. It is already referenced in .gitignore, suggesting partial adoption. Add to .Brewfile, add shell activation to .zsh/mise.zsh, and evaluate whether to deprecate individual version manager files (pyenv.zsh, nvm.zsh, rbenv.zsh). Keep existing managers until fully migrated.

title: Add direnv to Brewfile and configure shell hook
description: direnv auto-loads and unloads environment variables from .envrc files when entering/leaving directories. Essential for managing per-project secrets and env config without polluting the global shell. Add to .Brewfile and add eval hook in .zsh/zzzz_after.zsh.

title: Update CLAUDE.md and README.md for new repo structure
description:
Rewrite CLAUDE.md and README.md to reflect the symlink-based structure, removing Ansible references and documenting the new workflow.

Steps:
- Step 1: Update CLAUDE.md 'What This Is' section — remove Ansible description, describe symlink approach
- Step 2: Update CLAUDE.md 'Commands' section — replace ansible commands with make install, make diff, make brew-sync, make brew-dump
- Step 3: Update CLAUDE.md 'Architecture' section — document dotfiles/, hosts/, install.sh, Makefile structure
- Step 4: Remove or replace Ansible-specific tags and uv/mise setup instructions in CLAUDE.md
- Step 5: Update (or create) README.md with: repo purpose, prerequisites, make install usage, Brewfile drift workflow, per-machine override instructions

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: CLAUDE.md contains no references to ansible/, uv, mise, or Ansible commands
- AC2: CLAUDE.md 'Commands' section accurately describes make targets and quick zsh copy
- AC3: README.md explains the symlink approach and how to set up a new machine
- AC4: Architecture section documents dotfiles/, hosts/<machine>/, install.sh, and Makefile

title: Remove vestigial directories: ansible/, system/, apps.md
description:
Delete the Ansible-based infrastructure and stale files that the symlink approach replaces.

Steps:
- Step 1: Delete ansible/ directory entirely
- Step 2: Delete system/ directory entirely
- Step 3: Delete apps.md (references stale tools like Atom and YADR)
- Step 4: Update .gitignore if it references removed paths
- Step 5: Verify keyboardio/ is untouched

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: ansible/ directory no longer exists in the repo
- AC2: system/ directory no longer exists in the repo
- AC3: apps.md no longer exists in the repo
- AC4: keyboardio/ is unchanged
- AC5: No dangling references to removed paths in remaining files

title: Create Makefile with install and brew targets
description:
Write a Makefile at repo root with five targets covering install, diff, and Brewfile drift management.

Steps:
- Step 1: Add 'make install' target → runs ./install.sh
- Step 2: Add 'make diff' target → runs git diff (live files are repo files)
- Step 3: Add 'make brew-install' target → runs brew bundle install --global
- Step 4: Add 'make brew-sync' target → dumps to /tmp/.Brewfile.current, diffs vs dotfiles/.Brewfile (read-only drift view)
- Step 5: Add 'make brew-dump' target → runs brew bundle dump --force (writes live state to ~/.Brewfile → repo file)

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: make install invokes ./install.sh
- AC2: make diff runs git diff
- AC3: make brew-install runs brew bundle install --global
- AC4: make brew-sync shows bidirectional drift between live brew state and dotfiles/.Brewfile without modifying anything
- AC5: make brew-dump writes live brew state to ~/.Brewfile (which is the symlinked repo file)

title: Create install.sh symlink installer
description:
Write install.sh at repo root. Detects machine via hostname -s, symlinks individual files (not directories), backs up conflicts, applies shared dotfiles then host-specific overrides.

Steps:
- Step 1: Detect machine name via hostname -s; check if hosts/<machine>/ exists
- Step 2: For each file under dotfiles/, compute target path (strip dotfiles/ prefix, prepend ~/); apply symlink logic
- Step 3: Symlink logic per file: (a) target absent → create parent dirs + symlink; (b) already symlink into this repo → skip; (c) existing file/foreign symlink → rename to <target>.bak.<timestamp> then symlink
- Step 4: After shared files, process hosts/<machine>/ with same logic, overriding shared files of same name
- Step 5: Script must be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: Running install.sh on mbp2022 creates symlinks for all files under dotfiles/ into ~/
- AC2: Running install.sh twice produces no errors and skips already-correct symlinks
- AC3: A pre-existing ~/file gets renamed to ~/file.bak.<timestamp> before the symlink is created
- AC4: Script does NOT install software, run brew bundle, or touch anything outside ~/
- AC5: If hosts/<machine>/ does not exist, only shared dotfiles are installed (no error)

title: Migrate dotfiles from ansible/ to dotfiles/ directory
description:
Move all shared config files from ansible/roles/workstations/files/ into a new dotfiles/ directory mirroring ~/. Also create hosts/mbp2022/ placeholder and dotfiles/.config/ghostty/ placeholder.

Steps:
- Step 1: Create dotfiles/ directory structure (dotfiles/.zshrc, dotfiles/.zsh/, dotfiles/.zsh.before/, dotfiles/.zsh.after/, dotfiles/.vimrc, dotfiles/.Brewfile, dotfiles/.config/ghostty/)
- Step 2: Move zsh/.zshrc → dotfiles/.zshrc
- Step 3: Move zsh/.zsh/*.zsh → dotfiles/.zsh/*.zsh
- Step 4: Move zsh/.zsh.before/ → dotfiles/.zsh.before/
- Step 5: Move zsh/.zsh.after/ → dotfiles/.zsh.after/
- Step 6: Move vim/.vimrc → dotfiles/.vimrc
- Step 7: Move homebrew/.Brewfile-mac → dotfiles/.Brewfile
- Step 8: Create hosts/mbp2022/ empty placeholder directory
- Step 9: Create dotfiles/.config/ghostty/ placeholder
- Step 10: Verify dotfiles/.zsh/secrets.zsh sources from ~/.secrets (never from the repo) — credentials must never be committed

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: All files from ansible/roles/workstations/files/ exist at their new paths under dotfiles/
- AC2: hosts/mbp2022/ directory exists (can be empty)
- AC3: dotfiles/.config/ghostty/ placeholder exists
- AC4: No files in the old ansible/ paths are missing from dotfiles/
- AC5: secrets.zsh (if present) sources credentials from ~/.secrets, not from any path inside the repo

After moving files, verify symlinks exist at their target locations and update ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md if it currently points at the ai-configs repo version.

title: Add tmux workspace template to dotfiles
description:
Add workspace.sh session template script to dotfiles, following the pattern from the tmux field guide.

Reference: file:///Users/dlstadther/Downloads/tmux-guide_1.html (Templates tab)

## File Layout

dotfiles/.config/tmux/templates/workspace.sh

Tracked in dotfiles and symlinked to ~/.config/tmux/templates/workspace.sh via install.sh.

## Layout

Single window, 3 panes:
- Left pane (~60% width): runs 'claude' on open
- Top-right pane: runs 'git status'
- Bottom-right pane: clean zsh prompt (cd to dir, no command)

## workspace.sh

Path: ~/.config/tmux/templates/workspace.sh

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Usage: workspace.sh <path> [window-name]
#
# <path>         Required. Working directory for the session/window.
# [window-name]  Optional. Overrides auto-generated window name.
#
# Behaviour:
#   New session     → create session + window named from dirname (or override)
#   Existing session → add new window; auto-increment name if no override given
#   Always focuses the newly created window.

set -euo pipefail

if [[ $# -lt 1 ]]; then
  echo "Usage: workspace.sh <path> [window-name]" >&2
  exit 1
fi

TARGET_DIR="$(realpath "$1")"
WINDOW_NAME_OVERRIDE="${2:-}"

if [[ ! -d "$TARGET_DIR" ]]; then
  echo "error: directory does not exist: $TARGET_DIR" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if ! tmux info &>/dev/null; then
  echo "error: tmux is not running." >&2
  exit 1
fi

DIRNAME="$(basename "$TARGET_DIR")"
SESSION_NAME="$DIRNAME"

next_window_name() {
  local session="$1"
  local base="$2"
  local candidate="$base"
  local n=2
  while tmux list-windows -t "$session" -F "#{window_name}" 2>/dev/null         | grep -qx "$candidate"; do
    candidate="${base}-${n}"
    (( n++ ))
  done
  echo "$candidate"
}

create_panes() {
  local session="$1"
  local window="$2"
  local dir="$3"

  # Right column (~40%), leaving left pane selected
  tmux split-window -t "${session}:${window}" -h -p 40 -c "$dir"

  # Split right column vertically — top for git, bottom for zsh
  tmux split-window -t "${session}:${window}.1" -v -c "$dir"

  # Left pane: Claude Code
  tmux send-keys -t "${session}:${window}.0" "claude" Enter

  # Top-right: git status
  tmux send-keys -t "${session}:${window}.1" "git status" Enter

  # Bottom-right: clean zsh (opened at dir via -c above)

  # Focus left
  tmux select-pane -t "${session}:${window}.0"
}

if ! tmux has-session -t "$SESSION_NAME" 2>/dev/null; then
  WINDOW_NAME="${WINDOW_NAME_OVERRIDE:-$DIRNAME}"
  tmux new-session -d -s "$SESSION_NAME" -n "$WINDOW_NAME" -c "$TARGET_DIR"
  create_panes "$SESSION_NAME" "$WINDOW_NAME" "$TARGET_DIR"
  tmux switch-client -t "$SESSION_NAME"
else
  WINDOW_NAME="${WINDOW_NAME_OVERRIDE:-$(next_window_name "$SESSION_NAME" "$DIRNAME")}"
  tmux new-window -t "$SESSION_NAME" -n "$WINDOW_NAME" -c "$TARGET_DIR"
  create_panes "$SESSION_NAME" "$WINDOW_NAME" "$TARGET_DIR"
  tmux select-window -t "${SESSION_NAME}:${WINDOW_NAME}"
fi

## Usage examples

# New project session:
workspace.sh ~/code/myproject

# Add window to existing session:
workspace.sh ~/code/myproject feature-branch

# Custom window name:
workspace.sh ~/code/myproject dev

## install.sh additions

- Create ~/.config/tmux/templates/ directory if it doesn't exist
- Symlink workspace.sh into ~/.config/tmux/templates/workspace.sh
- Make script executable (chmod +x)

## Acceptance Criteria

- workspace.sh present in dotfiles and symlinked to ~/.config/tmux/templates/workspace.sh
- Script is executable
- Running workspace.sh on a new directory creates a named tmux session with 3-pane layout
- Running workspace.sh on an existing session name adds a new window instead of failing
- Left pane auto-starts claude, top-right runs git status

title: Add tmux persistence scripts to dotfiles
description:
Add the three tmux persistence bin scripts and bootstrap example to dotfiles, following the pattern from the tmux field guide.

Reference: file:///Users/dlstadther/Downloads/tmux-guide_1.html (Persistence tab)

## File Layout

dotfiles/.config/tmux/bin/tmux-restore
dotfiles/.config/tmux/bin/tmux-bootstrap
dotfiles/.config/tmux/bin/tmux-start
dotfiles/.config/tmux/bootstrap.example.sh

All bin scripts should be tracked in dotfiles and symlinked into ~/.config/tmux/bin/ via install.sh.
bootstrap.sh is machine-local and gitignored (only bootstrap.example.sh is tracked).
Add ~/.config/tmux/bin to PATH in dotfiles/.zsh/0_path.zsh or equivalent.

## tmux-restore

Path: ~/.config/tmux/bin/tmux-restore
Purpose: trigger tmux-resurrect restore from CLI without needing to be in tmux and hit a key binding.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Trigger tmux-resurrect restore from the command line.
# Must be run from inside a running tmux session.

if ! tmux info &>/dev/null; then
  echo "error: tmux is not running. Start tmux first." >&2
  exit 1
fi

RESURRECT_DIR="${XDG_DATA_HOME:-$HOME/.local/share}/tmux/resurrect"
RESURRECT_SCRIPT="$HOME/.tmux/plugins/tmux-resurrect/scripts/restore.sh"

if [[ ! -f "$RESURRECT_SCRIPT" ]]; then
  RESURRECT_SCRIPT="$HOME/.config/tmux/plugins/tmux-resurrect/scripts/restore.sh"
fi

if [[ ! -f "$RESURRECT_SCRIPT" ]]; then
  echo "error: tmux-resurrect not found. Is the plugin installed?" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "Restoring tmux session from last snapshot..."
tmux run-shell "$RESURRECT_SCRIPT"
echo "Done. Use prefix + Ctrl+r inside tmux if this had no effect."

## tmux-bootstrap

Path: ~/.config/tmux/bin/tmux-bootstrap
Purpose: run the machine-local bootstrap.sh to create always-on sessions. Safe to run multiple times (sessions that exist are skipped).

#!/usr/bin/env bash
if ! tmux info &>/dev/null; then
  echo "error: tmux is not running. Start tmux first." >&2
  exit 1
fi

BOOTSTRAP="$HOME/.config/tmux/bootstrap.sh"

if [[ ! -f "$BOOTSTRAP" ]]; then
  echo "error: $BOOTSTRAP not found." >&2
  echo "  Copy bootstrap.example.sh to bootstrap.sh and fill it in." >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "Running bootstrap..."
bash "$BOOTSTRAP"
echo "Bootstrap complete."

## tmux-start

Path: ~/.config/tmux/bin/tmux-start
Purpose: combo command — restore then bootstrap. Call once after reboot.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
if ! tmux info &>/dev/null; then
  echo "error: tmux is not running." >&2
  echo "  Start tmux first: tmux new-session -d -s main" >&2
  exit 1
fi

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"

echo "==> Step 1: Restoring sessions..."
"$SCRIPT_DIR/tmux-restore"

sleep 2

echo ""
echo "==> Step 2: Running bootstrap..."
"$SCRIPT_DIR/tmux-bootstrap"

echo ""
echo "All done. Attach with: tmux attach"

## bootstrap.example.sh

Path: ~/.config/tmux/bootstrap.example.sh
Purpose: template for machine-local session setup. Tracked in dotfiles; user copies to bootstrap.sh on each machine and fills in.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Machine-local bootstrap — copy to bootstrap.sh and customise.
# bootstrap.sh is gitignored. This example file is tracked instead.
#
# Run via: tmux-bootstrap  or  tmux-start

TEMPLATES="$HOME/.config/tmux/templates"

# Example: monitoring session
if ! tmux has-session -t "monitor" 2>/dev/null; then
  tmux new-session -d -s "monitor" -c "$HOME"
  tmux rename-window -t "monitor:1" "sys"
  tmux send-keys -t "monitor:sys" "btop" Enter
fi

# Example: notes session
if ! tmux has-session -t "notes" 2>/dev/null; then
  tmux new-session -d -s "notes" -c "$HOME/notes"
  tmux rename-window -t "notes:1" "notes"
fi

# Example: project session via workspace template
if ! tmux has-session -t "myproject" 2>/dev/null; then
  bash "$TEMPLATES/workspace.sh" "$HOME/projects/myproject"
fi

## .gitignore addition

Add to dotfiles/.gitignore (or the relevant ignore file):
.config/tmux/bootstrap.sh

## install.sh additions

- Symlink all three bin scripts into ~/.config/tmux/bin/ (create dir if needed, chmod +x)
- Symlink bootstrap.example.sh into ~/.config/tmux/bootstrap.example.sh
- Do NOT symlink bootstrap.sh

## Acceptance Criteria

- All three bin scripts present in dotfiles and symlinked to ~/.config/tmux/bin/
- Scripts are executable (chmod +x)
- bootstrap.example.sh tracked and symlinked
- bootstrap.sh in .gitignore
- ~/.config/tmux/bin on PATH
- tmux-start runs without errors when tmux is running

title: Configure tmux dotfiles
description:
Iterative setup of tmux config in dotfiles, worked through together with user to get preferences right.

Scope: add/update dotfiles/.tmux.conf (or equivalent) with user-preferred settings.

Known items to address:
- Add tmux-specific zsh aliases (e.g. attach, new session shortcuts)

Discovery reference: file:///Users/dlstadther/Downloads/tmux-guide_1.html

Guide sections and disposition:
- Core Concepts: background only, no action needed
- Commands (key bindings): reference during config work
- tmux.conf baseline:
  - KEEP ctrl+b prefix (do NOT remap)
  - ADD: mouse on, history-limit 50000, base-index 1, pane-base-index 1, renumber-windows on, escape-time 0, focus-events on, true color terminal settings
  - ADD: vi copy mode (mode-keys vi, v to select, y to pbcopy)
  - ADD: pipe-friendly splits (| for vertical, - for horizontal, both open at current path)
  - ADD: vim-style pane navigation (hjkl after prefix)
  - ADD: repeatable pane resize (HJKL with -r)
  - ADD: prefix + r to reload config
  - ADD: detach-on-destroy off, bind x kill-pane (skip confirm)
  - SKIP: status-position top (decide during session)
- Plugins (via TPM):
  - ADD: tmux-sensible (quality of life defaults)
  - ADD: tmux-yank (clipboard, no config needed)
  - ADD: tmux-resurrect + tmux-continuum (persistence):
      set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on'
      set -g @resurrect-strategy-nvim 'session'
      set -g @continuum-restore 'on'
      set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'
  - ADD: dracula/tmux theme:
      set -g @dracula-plugins "git battery cpu-usage ram-usage time"
      set -g @dracula-show-powerline true
      set -g @dracula-show-flags true
      set -g @dracula-show-left-icon session
      set -g @dracula-border-contrast true
      set -g @dracula-git-show-current-symbol ✓
      set -g @dracula-git-show-diff-symbol !
      set -g @dracula-show-timezone false
      set -g @dracula-time-format "%H:%M"
      set -g @dracula-show-battery-status true
      Note: do NOT set status-left/status-right manually — Dracula owns those
  - ADD: tmux-peacock (per-project window/pane colorization):
      https://github.com/imomaliev/tmux-peacock
      set -g @plugin 'imomaliev/tmux-peacock'
  - ADD: SessionX (fuzzy session picker via TPM):
      set -g @plugin 'omerxx/tmux-sessionx'
      set -g @sessionx-bind 'o'
      set -g @sessionx-window-height '85%'
      set -g @sessionx-window-width '75%'
      set -g @sessionx-preview-location 'right'
      set -g @sessionx-preview-ratio '55%'
      set -g @sessionx-filter-current 'false'
      set -g @sessionx-zoxide-mode 'on'
  - ADD: tmux-notify (notifications when long-running commands complete):
      set -g @plugin 'rickstaa/tmux-notify'
  - SKIP: vim-tmux-navigator (only relevant if using neovim splits)
- Persistence scripts and workspace templates: tracked in separate issues
close_reason: tmux.conf and tmux.zsh added with all specified settings and plugins

title: Audit and update .gitignore for post-ansible repo
description: The repo previously used Ansible but has since migrated away from it. The .gitignore likely contains Ansible-specific entries (e.g. ansible/.venv, *.retry, inventory secrets) that are no longer relevant. Audit the current .gitignore, remove stale Ansible-era entries, and ensure current tooling (mise, uv, install.sh) is properly covered.

title: Audit current machine config drift against repo
description: Check current machine's live settings (Homebrew packages, Zsh config, dotfiles, tool versions) against what's defined in the repo. Identify any drift — packages installed but not in Brewfile, configs in ~ not symlinked from repo, or repo files not deployed. Goal: catch manual changes made outside of the install/Ansible workflow before they're lost.
close_reason: Audited all live config against repo: Brewfile updated from live state, added mise.zsh, fixed 0_path.zsh, brought in .gitconfig, tmux.conf, and ssh config to hosts/mbp2022. Remaining drift is unlinked files — resolved by running install.sh.
